title: Wildlife Corridor Flow-Balance: Length Generalization
strategies: constant / majority-of-train predictor: emit the single most frequent training label for every log (reproduces the evaluator's baseline; captures none of the length generalization)

memorization / 1-nearest-neighbour in raw tag-count space: copy the label of the closest training log -- fits the short in-distribution logs but collapses on the long out-of-distribution logs whose counts dwarf anything seen in training

rule recovery: enumerate the tiny integer weight space, keep every per-tag flow rule consistent with all training band-constraints, and pick the simplest (min-L1) one, then apply the exact rule at any length -- extrapolates to long logs but the tolerance band leaves slack so it is not perfect (headroom)

hypothesis averaging / margin selection: over the set of training-consistent weight rules, predict by majority vote or pick the rule that maximizes classification margin, trading off among rules the short logs cannot disambiguate
